examples.title: Simple Three-Point Estimation (PERT)
examples.description: Pseudocode for calculating expected effort using optimistic, pessimistic, and most likely estimates
examples.explanation: Three-point estimation uses three values (optimistic, most likely, pessimistic) to calculate a weighted average. The PERT formula gives more weight to the most likely estimate, providing a more realistic expectation than simple averaging.

examples.title: Bottom-Up Estimation Algorithm
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating the bottom-up estimation process
examples.code:
FUNCTION bottomUpEstimate(workPackages)
 totalEstimate = 0
 estimates = EMPTY_LIST
 
 FOR EACH package IN workPackages
 // Estimate each work package in detail
 packageEstimate = estimatePackage(package)
 
 // Add contingency based on risk assessment
 riskContingency = calculateRiskContingency(package)
 adjustedEstimate = packageEstimate + riskContingency
 
 estimates.add(adjustedEstimate)
 totalEstimate = totalEstimate + adjustedEstimate
 END FOR
 
 // Apply overall project contingency
 overallContingency = totalEstimate * PROJECT_RISK_PERCENTAGE
 finalEstimate = totalEstimate + overallContingency
 
 RETURN finalEstimate, estimates
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This algorithm shows how bottom-up estimation works by estimating each work package individually, adding risk contingencies at both package and project level, then aggregating to get total estimate.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n = number of work packages

examples.title: Re-estimation During Project Execution
examples.description: Algorithm for iterative estimation as project progresses
examples.code:
FUNCTION reEstimateProject(projectState)
 originalEstimate = projectState.originalEstimate
 completedWork = projectState.completedWork
 actualEffort = projectState.actualEffortSpent
 remainingWork = projectState.estimatedRemainingWork
 
 // Calculate productivity factor
 productivityRatio = completedWork / actualEffort
 
 // Adjust remaining work estimate based on actual productivity
 adjustedRemainingWork = remainingWork / productivityRatio
 
 // Calculate new total estimate
 newTotalEstimate = actualEffort + adjustedRemainingWork
 
 // Calculate confidence based on completion percentage
 completionPercentage = completedWork / (completedWork + remainingWork)
 uncertaintyRange = calculateUncertainty(completionPercentage)
 
 // Return estimate with range
 lowerBound = newTotalEstimate * (1 - uncertaintyRange)
 upperBound = newTotalEstimate * (1 + uncertaintyRange)
 
 RETURN newTotalEstimate, lowerBound, upperBound
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates how to re-estimate project effort as work progresses. By comparing actual productivity to original estimates, we can adjust remaining work estimates and provide more accurate projections with confidence ranges.
